title: How to proceed with SRS airbag system failure diagnosis
section: Driver assistance SRS airbags seat belts

blocks.text: How to proceed with SRS airbag system failure diagnosis
The SRS warning light (A) indicates when there is a problem with the functioning of the SRS airbag system.

If there is no problem with the SRS airbag system, when the ignition is turned to the ON mode, the SRS warning light illuminates for 6 seconds and then goes off. If the light does not turn off, there is a problem with the system.

If the SRS warning light is on, check for DTCs, and if DTCs are present, perform failure diagnosis as indicated by the failure diagnosis table by DTC; if no DTCs are present, perform failure diagnosis by the failure diagnosis table by symptom.

items: Proceed with the work while observing work precautions. ​
A fully charged 12V battery should be used. If the battery is undercharged, the failure diagnosis may not come out correctly.

When proceeding with failure diagnosis related to wire breakage, since the failure may be due to poor terminal contact, the state of connection shall be checked when disconnecting the coupler, and the disconnected terminals shall be visually inspected.

blocks.text: This vehicle uses a DENSO or TRW SRS unit depending on the specifications. Since the system varies depending on the manufacturer, check the ECU manufacturer displayed in the data list using HDS before starting the work.

items.text: When contacting the DENSO SRS unit coupler (A) with the dedicated tool (pin probe male) or jumper wire, be sure to make contact from the cord side of the coupler. Do not connect from the terminal side or modify the coupler.
For failure diagnosis, use a commercially available digital circuit tester (B) that meets the standards and insert it from the cord side of the coupler via a dedicated tool (pin probe male).
When contacting the TRW SRS unit coupler (A) with the dedicated tool (pin probe male) or jumper wire, be sure to make contact through the inspection hole (B) on the coupler terminal side.
For failure diagnosis, use a commercially available digital circuit tester (A) that meets the standards and insert it into the inspection hole of the coupler via a dedicated tool (pin probe male).
When contacting the waterproof coupler (A) with the dedicated tool (pin probe male) or jumper wire, be sure to make contact through the inspection hole (B) on the coupler terminal side.
For failure diagnosis, use a commercially available digital circuit tester (A) that meets the standards and insert it into the inspection hole of the coupler via a dedicated tool (pin probe male).
When contacting the inflator coupler (A) with the dedicated tool (pin probe male) or jumper wire, be sure to make contact through the inspection hole (B) on the coupler terminal side.
For failure diagnosis, use a commercially available digital circuit tester (A) that meets the standards and insert it into the inspection hole of the coupler via a dedicated tool (pin probe male).

When contacting the unsealed coupler with the dedicated tool (back probe adapter) or jumper wire, make contact from the cord side of the coupler. Do not connect from the terminal side or modify the coupler.
For failure diagnosis, use a commercially available digital circuit tester (A) and insert it from the cord side of the coupler via a dedicated tool (back probe adapter).

blocks.text: The coupler that connects to the SRS unit manufactured by TRW is equipped with an automatic short-circuit mechanism. This automatic short-circuit mechanism is installed in the circuit of the airbag and seat belt pretensioner, and when the coupler is disconnected from the SRS unit, it shorts the circuit on the deployment side to prevent malfunction of the airbag. When it is necessary to remove the short circuit for failure diagnosis, a special tool (SRS short canceler) is inserted into the upper side of the two terminals designated from the connection side of the coupler to remove the short circuit between the terminals.
Precautions when using the dedicated tool (SRS Short Canceller)

items.text: Before use, wash off dirt with a mild detergent and remove water droplets by air blow.
Before use, check that there is no deformation or damage to the insert of the tool.
Insert straight into the insertion hole on the upper side of the specified terminal from the terminal side of the coupler, and do not move it except in the insertion direction after insertion.
After use, the tool must be removed from the coupler. Also, inspect the coupler to ensure that no debris from the tool remains in the coupler before connecting it.

blocks.text: 2. Connect the HDS (Honda Diagnostics System) to the data link coupler (A) on the left side of the driver's seat. For information on the use of HDS, refer to the HDS instruction manual. 3. Turn ignition to ON mode. 4. Verify that HDS communicates with the vehicle and the SRS unit. If it does not communicate, Inspect data link circuits The following is a list of the most common problems with the 5. Select a trouble code from the HDS DTC menu and check the DTC. 6. Turn the ignition to the OFF (LOCK) mode and wait at least 10 seconds. 7. Proceed to failure diagnosis.

2. Connect HDS to the data link coupler (A) on the left side of the driver's seat. For information on the use of HDS, refer to the HDS instruction manual. 3. Turn ignition to ON mode. 4. Verify that HDS communicates with the vehicle and the SRS unit. If it does not communicate, Inspect data link circuits The following is a list of the most common problems with the 5. Select Clear from the HDS Test Mode menu to clear DTCs. 6. Turn the ignition to the OFF (LOCK) mode and wait at least 10 seconds.

If any failure has occurred in the past but there is no failure at present, the SRS warning light comes on because it is memorized as a transient failure. if the DTC is inspected and determined to be a transient failure, the following procedure is used to check it.

2. Turn the ignition to the ON mode. the SRS warning light illuminates for 6 seconds and then goes out. 3. Apply vibration (manual movement) to the wire harness and coupler section in Step 2. Or, apply an external factor such as a driving test (sudden acceleration, sudden deceleration, turning). If the system abnormality is reproduced, the SRS warning light will go from off to on. 4. When the SRS warning light changes to on. ‐1. Record the nature of the external factors at the time of the lighting condition. ‐2. Clear DTC after confirming and recording DTC. ‐3. Identify the cause of the failure based on the contents of external factors and confirmation of DTC (If the cause cannot be identified in one inspection, repeat inspection steps 2, 3, and 4 several times). 5. If the SRS warning light does not change to on, the system is currently considered normal and the work is completed. However, if the vehicle history shows repeated transient failures of the same event, all presumed failed parts should be replaced.
